steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. Map what each sentence does: sentence 1 defines community science; sentence 2 lists three specific benefits; sentence 3 names a real study that used this approach. The correct answer must capture all three moves in order.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘identifies a particular approach … lists some benefits … mentions a study’: matches exactly — definition in sentence 1, three-item benefit list in sentence 2, Merrill’s study in sentence 3. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘argues for a new approach … public’s opinion’: the passage makes no argument and never mentions public opinion. “Argues” and “public opinion” are invented. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘development of a type of collaboration … suggests future projects’: the passage does not trace a history of how community science developed, nor does it suggest any future projects. Both elements are fabricated. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘introduces a study … presents the study’s results’: the passage introduces community science as an approach, not Merrill’s study specifically, and no results are ever given. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
